enonce.texte: Match the shopping and services vocabulary words on the left with their correct definitions on the right. Write the letter of the correct answer.

questions.texte: Match each term with its definition.

sousQuestions.reponse: c. A large self-service shop selling foods and household goods.
e. A person who buys goods or services from a shop.
a. A written acknowledgment that payment has been made.
b. Buying goods or services over the internet.
d. A regular gathering of people for the purchase and sale of provisions, often outdoors.
f. The quality of being easy, suitable, or handy for use.
g. A statement of money owed for goods or services supplied.

intitule: Fill in the Blanks with Shopping Vocabulary

enonce.texte:
Complete each sentence with the correct word from the box below.

Word box: supermarket, receipt, customer, market, waiter, shelves, online shopping, bill, rent, landlord

questions.texte: Identify and write the correct vocabulary word to complete each sentence.
questions.reponse: 1. supermarket, 2. receipt, 3. customer, 4. market, 5. online shopping, 6. shelves, 7. waiter, 8. bill

sousQuestions.texte: I need to buy rice, plantains, and fish, so I will go to the _______ near my house.
sousQuestions.reponse: supermarket

sousQuestions.texte: After paying for my groceries, the cashier gave me a _______ to prove my purchase.
sousQuestions.reponse: receipt

sousQuestions.texte: The _______ asked the shopkeeper for the price of the tomatoes.
sousQuestions.reponse: customer

sousQuestions.texte: Every Saturday, the open-air _______ is full of people selling vegetables and clothes.
sousQuestions.reponse: market

sousQuestions.texte: Because she was busy, my mother prefers _______; she orders everything from her phone.
sousQuestions.reponse: online shopping

sousQuestions.texte: The products were arranged neatly on the wooden _______ in the store.
sousQuestions.reponse: shelves

sousQuestions.texte: The _______ took our order and brought us eru and plantains.
sousQuestions.reponse: waiter

sousQuestions.texte: Could we have the _______, please? We are ready to pay for our meal.
sousQuestions.reponse: bill

intitule: Creating a Dialogue at the Market (Role Play)

enonce.texte: Imagine you are at the Mokolo Market in Yaoundé. You want to buy 2 kg of plantains, 1 kg of fish, and 3 bottles of water. The seller gives you prices. Create a complete dialogue between you (the customer) and the seller. Use at least five vocabulary words from Lesson 24 (customer, market, bill, receipt, price, change, etc.).

questions.texte: Write a realistic dialogue (8-10 exchanges) between a customer and a seller at a market.
questions.reponse:
Customer: Good morning! How much are your plantains? 
Seller: Good morning! They are 200 CFA per kg. 
Customer: I would like 2 kg, please. 
Seller: That will be 400 CFA for plantains. 
Customer: Also, how much is this fish? 
Seller: The fish is 1500 CFA per kg. 
Customer: OK, I'll take 1 kg. 
Seller: So plantains (400) + fish (1500) = 1900 CFA. Anything else? 
Customer: Yes, three bottles of water, please. 
Seller: Water is 300 CFA each, so total 900 CFA for water. Your total bill is 2800 CFA. 
Customer: Here is 3000 CFA. 
Seller: Thank you. Here is your change (200 CFA) and your receipt. 
Customer: Thank you. Have a good day!

enonce.texte:
Read the situation and answer the questions using comparative and superlative structures (from Chapter 2, Lesson 6).

Situation: You need to buy a new school bag. You have three options:

  • Option A: Local market – Price: 3,000 CFA – Quality: Medium – Distance: 1 km – No return policy
  • Option B: Supermarket – Price: 6,000 CFA – Quality: High – Distance: 5 km – 7-day return policy
  • Option C: Online shopping – Price: 5,000 CFA (including delivery) – Quality: High – Distance: 0 km – 14-day return policy

questions.texte: Compare the prices of the three options. Write one sentence using a comparative adjective.
questions.reponse: The local market is cheaper than the supermarket and online shopping. OR The supermarket is more expensive than the local market.

questions.texte: Which option offers the highest quality? Write a sentence using a superlative adjective.
questions.reponse: The supermarket and online shopping offer the highest quality. OR The best quality is from the supermarket and online shopping.

questions.texte: Justify which option you would choose for buying the school bag. Give two reasons using vocabulary from Lesson 24 (e.g., convenience, receipt, customer service, return policy).
questions.reponse: I would choose online shopping because it is more convenient (I don't need to travel) and it has the longest return policy (14 days), which protects me as a customer. Although it is more expensive than the market, the quality is high.

enonce.texte:
Below is a receipt from a supermarket. Find and correct 5 errors. The errors can be in vocabulary, spelling, grammar, or math.

Receipt:

Supermarket ABC - Douala
Date: 12/05/2025

Item: Rice (2 kg) - 1000 CFA
Item: Fish (0.5 kg) - 750 CFA
Item: Plantain (3 pcs) - 600 CFA
Item: Botle of water - 300 CFA
Subtotal: 2650 CFA
Tax: 350 CFA
Total bill: 3000 CFA
Payment: 3500 CFA
Change: 400 CFA

Thank you for shopping! Keep your receipt for any return.

questions.texte: Identify and correct five errors on this receipt.
questions.reponse: 1. 'Botle' → 'Bottle' (spelling error); 2. Subtotal calculation: 1000+750+600+300 = 2650 CFA (correct, no error here but verify); 3. Total bill: 2650 + 350 = 3000 CFA (math is correct); 4. Change: 3500 - 3000 = 500 CFA (not 400 CFA) → Error; 5. Missing unit for plantain (pcs could be written as 'pieces') → minor error; Alternative errors: No 'customer name', no 'receipt number'.

sousQuestions.reponse: 'Botle' should be 'Bottle'.
The change is wrong. 3500 CFA paid - 3000 CFA total = 500 CFA change, not 400 CFA.
The receipt does not include a receipt number, cashier name, or customer loyalty information (optional but standard).
No major grammar error, but 'for any return' could be improved to 'for any returns' or 'for returns'.
The unit 'pcs' is informal; it should be 'pieces' for standard receipts.

enonce.texte:
Last week, you bought a perishable item (fish) from a supermarket. When you opened it at home, it smelled bad and had expired. You have the receipt. Write a formal complaint letter to the supermarket manager. Use the following structure and vocabulary from Lesson 24.

Vocabulary to include: receipt, customer, expiry date, perishable, refund, complaint, bill, quality, service

Letter structure: Your address, date, manager's address, subject line, body (problem + proof + request), closing.

questions.texte: Write a formal complaint letter (150-200 words) to the supermarket manager.
questions.reponse:
**Student's answer should resemble:**

[Your Name]
P.O. Box 123
Yaoundé, Cameroon

March 15, 2025

The Manager
FreshMart Supermarket
Douala, Cameroon

**Subject: Complaint about expired fish purchased on March 10, 2025**

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to formally complain about a product I purchased from your supermarket on March 10, 2025. As a regular customer, I bought a pack of fish (perishable item) with my receipt number #4521.

When I arrived home and opened the package, I noticed a very bad smell. I checked the expiry date on the label, and it showed that the fish had expired two days before my purchase. This is unacceptable for a supermarket that claims to offer high-quality service.

I request a full refund of 2,500 CFA that I paid for this product. I have attached my receipt as proof of purchase. I also suggest that your staff check expiry dates regularly to avoid such problems for other customers.

Thank you for understanding. I look forward to your prompt response.

Yours faithfully,
[Your signature]
[Your printed name]
